title: The High Price of Our Habits: How Top Consumers Impact the Planet
subtitle: New research shows that the world's wealthiest individuals cause trillions of dollars in environmental damage every year.

summary.en: A new study reveals that the world's top 10% of consumers cause up to $5.7 trillion in environmental damage annually. This cost comes from high levels of food and energy consumption, including red meat and fossil fuels. Researchers found that this group has a massive impact on climate change and the loss of nature. While many of these people live in wealthy nations, high-consuming households in emerging economies are also catching up. Experts suggest that taxing luxury goods and investments could help solve these problems.
summary.zh: 一項最新研究顯示，全球前百分之十的消費者每年造成高達五點七兆美元的環境損害。這些代價源於高水平的食物與能源消費，包括紅肉攝取與化石燃料使用。研究人員發現，該群體對氣候變遷與自然喪失有著巨大影響。雖然這些人多居住在富裕國家，但新興經濟體中的高消費家庭也正迅速跟進。專家建議，針對奢侈品與投資課稅將有助於解決這些問題。

paragraphs.en: A new study has revealed the massive environmental cost caused by the world’s most wealthy and high-consuming people. According to The Guardian, the top 10% of the global population is responsible for up to $5.7 trillion in environmental damage every year. This figure is so large that it is greater than the economy of every country in the world, except for the United States and China.
paragraphs.zh: 一項最新研究揭露了全球最富有且消費力最高的人群所造成的巨大環境代價。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，全球前百分之十的人口每年造成的環境損害高達五點七兆美元。這個數字龐大到超越了世界上除了美國與中國以外，任何單一國家的經濟規模。

paragraphs.en: This report, published in the journal Communications Sustainability, was created by researchers at the University of Oxford and the University of Leiden. It highlights how the world’s economic priorities are currently focused on activities that harm the Earth’s life-support systems. The researchers noted that this damage bill is even higher than the total funding needed to fix the current climate and biodiversity crises.
paragraphs.zh: 這份發表在《通訊永續性》（Communications Sustainability）期刊上的報告，是由牛津大學（University of Oxford）與萊頓大學（University of Leiden）的研究人員所撰寫。報告強調，目前的全球經濟優先事項過度集中於損害地球生命維持系統的活動上。研究人員指出，這筆損害帳單甚至高於解決當前氣候與生物多樣性危機所需的總資金。

paragraphs.en: What exactly are these “mega-consumers” doing to cause such high costs? The study points to two main areas: food and energy. In terms of food, the consumption of red meat is a major driver of deforestation, which is the clearing of forests for land use. Regarding energy, the high costs come from frequent flights and the heating or cooling of large homes. These activities usually rely on burning fossil fuels—natural resources like coal, oil, and gas that release harmful gases into the atmosphere when burned.
paragraphs.zh: 這些「超級消費者」究竟做了什麼導致如此高昂的代價？研究指出兩個主要領域：食物與能源。在食物方面，紅肉的消費是導致森林砍伐的主要推手，也就是為了土地利用而清除森林的行為。在能源方面，高昂的代價來自於頻繁的飛行，以及大型住宅的冷暖氣使用。這些活動通常依賴燃燒化石燃料，即煤炭、石油與天然氣等天然資源，這些資源在燃燒時會向大氣釋放有害氣體。

paragraphs.en: Geographically, these high-consuming individuals are mostly found in the global north. The Guardian reports that this group includes more than half of the population in the United States and about 40% to 45% of people in the European Union. However, the report also warns that high-consuming households in emerging economies are catching up quickly. For example, the average environmental damage caused by the top 10% of people in China has now overtaken that of the top 10% in Germany.
paragraphs.zh: 從地理位置來看，這些高消費族群大多位於全球北方國家。《衛報》報導指出，該群體包含美國超過半數的人口，以及歐盟約百分之四十至四十五的人口。然而，報告也警告，新興經濟體中的高消費家庭正迅速追趕上來。例如，中國前百分之十的人口所造成的平均環境損害，現已超越德國前百分之十的人口。

paragraphs.en: Biodiversity loss—the decline in the variety of plants and animals on Earth—accounts for the largest share of this damage, making up over half of the total bill. The climate emergency is responsible for most of the remaining impact. The authors of the study argue that these two issues should be addressed together rather than as separate problems. They also warn that these numbers are likely conservative, meaning the true cost could be even higher. This is because the study only looked at direct consumption and did not include the environmental impact of investments, such as stocks and bonds.
paragraphs.zh: 生物多樣性喪失，即地球上動植物種類的減少，佔了這筆損害帳單的最大份額，超過總額的一半。氣候緊急狀態則造成了其餘大部分的影響。研究作者主張，這兩個議題應被視為一體，而非分開處理。他們也警告，這些數據可能屬於保守估計，意味著真實代價可能更高。這是因為該研究僅檢視了直接消費，並未包含投資（如股票與債券）所帶來的環境影響。

paragraphs.en: Paul Behrens, a professor at the University of Oxford and a co-author of the study, explained that a large part of a wealthy person’s carbon footprint—the total amount of greenhouse gases caused by their actions—comes from what they own rather than just how they live. A separate study by Greenpeace recently estimated that the assets owned by the world’s richest 1% are associated with a quarter of global emissions and cause nearly $1 trillion of climate damage each year.
paragraphs.zh: 牛津大學教授兼研究共同作者保羅·貝倫斯（Paul Behrens）解釋說，富人碳足跡（即其行為所產生的溫室氣體總量）中，很大一部分來自於他們所擁有的資產，而不僅僅是他們的生活方式。綠色和平組織（Greenpeace）近期的一項獨立研究估計，全球最富有百分之一人口所持有的資產，與全球四分之一的排放量有關，且每年造成近一兆美元的氣候損害。

paragraphs.en: Despite the scale of the problem, experts believe that this group also has the most power to create change. Because these individuals are often investors, employers, and trend-makers, they have a unique ability to shape markets and influence how companies operate. The report suggests that governments could use this information to create new policies. By placing taxes on luxury goods, wealth, and carbon emissions, governments could reduce pollution while raising money to support a transition to more sustainable energy.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管問題規模龐大，專家認為這群人也最有能力創造改變。由於這些人通常是投資者、雇主與趨勢引領者，他們擁有獨特的能力來塑造市場並影響企業的運作方式。報告建議政府可利用這些資訊制定新政策。透過對奢侈品、財富與碳排放課稅，政府可以在減少污染的同時，籌集資金以支持向更永續能源的轉型。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the study serves as a call to action for policymakers to apply the “polluter-pays” principle. This is the idea that those who cause environmental damage should be the ones to pay for the cleanup and the transition to a greener economy. As the world faces increasing climate disruption, the actions of the top 10% of consumers will remain a central part of the conversation regarding how to protect the planet for the future.
paragraphs.zh: 最終，這項研究呼籲決策者落實「污染者付費」原則。此概念主張，造成環境損害的人應當負責支付清理費用，並負擔轉型至綠色經濟的成本。隨著世界面臨日益嚴重的氣候破壞，前百分之十消費者的行為，將持續成為保護地球未來相關討論的核心議題。

keyTerms.definitionEn: The total amount of cost or damage caused by an activity.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 帳單；這裡指造成的損害總額。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「帳單」或「法案」，這裡指環境損害的代價。文中：The researchers noted that this damage bill is even higher than the total funding needed to fix the current climate and biodiversity crises.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A factor that causes a particular phenomenon to happen or develop.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 驅動力；導致某事發生的主要因素。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「司機」，這裡指造成環境問題的推手。文中：In terms of food, the consumption of red meat is a major driver of deforestation, which is the clearing of forests for land use.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Estimated at a low or cautious level, likely to be lower than the actual amount.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 保守的；這裡指估計值偏低、謹慎的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「保守派的」，這裡指數據估算得比較謹慎。文中：They also warn that these numbers are likely conservative, meaning the true cost could be even higher.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Things of value that a person or company owns, such as property or investments.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 資產；個人或公司擁有的有價值之物。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於財經領域，指個人或企業擁有的財產。文中：A separate study by Greenpeace recently estimated that the assets owned by the world’s richest 1% are associated with a quarter of global emissions and cause nearly $1 trillion of climate damage each year.

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: ‘Mega-consumers’ of food and energy cost environment $5.7tn a year, study finds

previousArticles.title: The High Price of High Consumption: How the Richest 10% Impact the Planet
previousArticles.summaryEn: A new study from the University of Oxford and the University of Leiden highlights the massive environmental cost of the world's top 10% of consumers. These individuals are responsible for up to $5.7 trillion in damage annually through their habits and investments. The research notes that this figure is larger than the economy of almost every country on Earth. Key drivers of this destruction include high levels of meat consumption and heavy reliance on fossil fuels for travel and home energy. Experts suggest that taxing these groups could help fund global efforts to protect nature and the climate.

quiz.question: According to the study, which of the following is true regarding the environmental damage caused by the top 10% of the global population?

options.A: It is exactly equal to the combined GDP of the United States and China.
options.B: It exceeds the total financial resources required to address current climate and biodiversity issues.
options.C: It is primarily caused by the investments and stocks held by these individuals.
options.D: It is currently lower than the damage caused by the bottom 90% of the population.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段明確指出，這項環境損害的金額甚至高於解決氣候與生物多樣性危機所需的總資金。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章僅說該金額大於世界上除美中以外的所有國家經濟體，並非等於美中總和。選項 C 錯誤，因為第五段提到該研究僅計算直接消費，未將投資納入計算。選項 D 文章並未提及，且邏輯上與前 10% 高消費者的定義不符。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the environmental impact of 'mega-consumers' in emerging economies?

options.A: They are currently causing less environmental damage than the average person in the global north.
options.B: Their environmental footprint is growing at a rate that is beginning to rival or exceed that of traditional high-consuming nations.
options.C: They are largely unaffected by government policies like luxury taxes or carbon emissions regulations.
options.D: They primarily contribute to environmental damage through the ownership of stocks and bonds rather than direct consumption.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段，新興經濟體的高消費群體正在快速追趕，例如中國前 10% 的環境損害已超過德國的前 10%，這暗示了其影響力正在與傳統已開發國家並駕齊驅。選項 A 與文意相反，文章提到他們正在追趕。選項 C 在文中未提及。選項 D 錯誤，因為文章第五段說明該研究目前僅關注直接消費，且未將投資納入中國等地的比較分析中。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'polluter-pays' principle imply?

options.A: Companies should be forced to shut down if they cause any pollution.
options.B: Wealthy individuals should be the only people allowed to use fossil fuels.
options.C: Those responsible for environmental harm should bear the financial burden of cleanup and sustainable transition.
options.D: Governments should pay for environmental damage using taxes collected from the general public.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章最後一段明確定義「污染者付費」原則為：造成環境損害的人應當為清理工作及轉型至綠色經濟支付費用。選項 A 過於極端，文中並未主張關閉公司。選項 B 與文意完全相反。選項 D 錯誤，因為該原則強調的是由「污染者」而非「大眾」來負擔成本，旨在透過稅收讓污染者承擔責任。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: The global population must stop all air travel and heating to save the planet.
options.B: Scientific research is the only way to solve the climate crisis effectively.
options.C: The environmental damage caused by the world's wealthiest is significant, but they also possess the influence to lead a transition toward sustainability.
options.D: Emerging economies are solely responsible for the current increase in global environmental damage.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心在於指出全球前 10% 高消費群體造成的巨大環境成本，並強調這些人同時具備投資者與決策者的身分，擁有推動市場轉型與政策改變的獨特潛力。選項 A 過於偏頗，僅是文中提到的部分細節。選項 B 並非文章主旨。選項 D 錯誤，文章雖提到新興經濟體正在追趕，但並未將責任全歸咎於他們，且強調的是全球高消費群體的共同影響。
